Blechnum species, such as Blechnum novae-zelandiae, are a type of fern that belongs to the Blechnaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in treating various conditions such as wounds and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Blechnum species include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids. Traditionally, Blechnum species are used to make herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and ointments, which are used for wound healing and skin conditions.
